summary: Good 1973 performance with a standout Dark Star, captured on a mixed‑source recording that has occasional mix quirks.
review: The show is praised for its classic ’73 vibe – not too fast and not too slow – with Dark Star singled out as a highlight. The recording is a blend of soundboard and audience sources; it’s listenable but suffers from odd mix issues (notably on Wharf Rat) and missing stage mics on El Paso.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance (average 4‑star reviews) balanced against a fair‑quality recording with mix problems, yielding an overall rating in the upper‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Some funny audio artifacts and poor drum mix on Wharf Rat; El Paso is a pure audience recording with stage mics off, indicating a mixed soundboard/audience source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical 1973 set—balanced tempos and energy (‘not too fast and not too slow’) that fans of the era will enjoy.

band_member_comments.Drums: Billy's mix is noted as poor on some tracks (especially Wharf Rat).
